channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: ICC opens inquiry into Hungary for failing to arrest Netanyahu
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — Judges at the International Criminal Court want Hungary to explain why it failed to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu when he visited Budapest earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dozens more possible jurors are screened in Weinstein retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Dozens more people are undergoing screening Thursday as potential jurors for Harvey Weinstein's New York rape retrial. The panel started taking shape Wednesday, when five women and four men were picked for the redo of the landmark #MeToo-era trial.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Arson attack on Pennsylvania governor's home evokes trauma for state's Jews
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A year ago, Carole Zawatsky was invited by Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro to a Passover Seder, held in the same room at the governor’s official residence that was devastated by an arsonist just hours after Shapiro hosted this year’s Jewish holiday feast.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, MARK SCOLFORO and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Family holds funeral for young mother, only to learn she's still alive
item.description: A New York family is navigating an emotional journey after learning their loved one, whom officials declared dead last year, is actually alive and living out of state. Shanita Hopkins reached out to Nexstar's WROC with a very unusual situation.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Garcia, KTLA 5

item.title: Former MLS player Aaron Boupendza dies after falling from building in China, soccer officials say
item.description: The Gabonese soccer federation announced Wednesday that Aaron Boupendza, a striker for its men's national team, has died after falling from the 11th floor of a building in China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: New York family told missing woman was found dead, only to learn she's living in Detroit
item.description: A New York family is navigating an emotional journey after learning their sister, whom officials declared dead last year, is actually alive and living out of state.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Garcia, ABC 4

item.title: Former OneRepublic bassist to take on California House Republican in tight district
item.description: Democrats are hoping pop rock star power will help oust a long-serving Republican east of Los Angeles in the fight for the U.S. House.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Blood, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Power blackout hits all of Puerto Rico as residents prepare for Easter weekend
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — A power blackout hit all of Puerto Rico on Wednesday as the heavily Catholic U.S. territory prepared to celebrate the Easter weekend.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: San Francisco's new mayor is starting to unite the fractured city
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Democrats nationally may be in turmoil, but liberals in San Francisco are hopeful the new mayor's collaborative approach will help solve entrenched problems in a city recently known for its bitter infighting and chaotic streets.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Arson attack probe at Pennsylvania governor's mansion looking into suspect's hatred of Josh Shapiro
item.description: HERSHEY, Pa. (AP) — Authorities investigating why a man set fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s official residence are probing whether the suspect was motivated by the Democrat's Jewish faith or positions on Israel’s war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NY prison guards beat an inmate to death then tried to cover it up, prosecutors say
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Ten New York prison guards were charged Wednesday in connection with the fatal beating of a 22-year-old inmate last month — including two charged with murder. It's the second time a group of correctional officers in the state was indicted for a death behind bars this year.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL and DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New York AG referred to DOJ for alleged mortgage fraud
item.description: If proven, the claims could lead to prosecution for one of New York’s highest-ranking officials, who has long argued that “no one is above the law.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Handyman named person of interest after missing woman's body found buried in concrete
item.description: A missing woman was found buried and partially encased in concrete more than a week after she went missing. Now, deputies are questioning her handyman for his possible role in her disappearance and death.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawyers file wrongful death notice against Idaho city for family of boy shot by police
item.description: A California law firm notified the city of Pocatello, Idaho, on Wednesday of its intention to file a federal wrongful death lawsuit on the behalf of the family of Victor Perez, a 17-year-old boy with intellectual disabilities who was shot multiple times by four police officers.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lost toddler found miles from home by dog that guided him to safety, sheriff says
item.description: When an Arizona toddler wandered away from home Monday, a team of deputies, search and rescue volunteers and rangers began their search. In the end, it was a protective pup who track down the 2-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: Tariffs will cost families nearly $5k per year, Kentucky governor says
item.description: "That's months upon months upon months of groceries. That's months upon months of rent. That might be your entire annual deductible if you've got private health insurance coverage," he said.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Calalang, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Erie's John Oliver responds to Last Week Tonight's John Oliver
item.description: (WJET/WFXP) -- It's a battle of the John Olivers. After Erie got some attention from Last Week Tonight with John Oliver, the president and CEO of VisitErie, John Oliver, posted a response to the TV host, inviting him to visit.
item.dc:creator: Drew Miller, NewsNation

item.title: MLB ump leaves game after foul ball hits him in the face
item.description: Veteran umpire Hunter Wendelstedt instantly hit the ground after he took a direct hit from the line smash off the bat of New York Mets center fielder Tyrone Taylor in the seventh inning.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Zoom service restored after outage affects thousands
item.description: Thousands of Zoom users reported issues accessing virtual meetings Wednesday, with the site confirming shortly after noon it was investigating the issue.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: 3 dead in multi-boat crash during Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama
item.description: A fatal boat crash during a Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Austin Franklin, Fox 5

item.title: Teens slashed adults who told them to be quiet during movie, police say
item.description: According to police, the boys, who were between the ages of 14 and 16, got upset after they were told to be quiet during a movie.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, NewsNation

item.title: ACLU sues Defense Department school system over banning race- and gender-related books
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union has sued the Department of Defense’s school system for children of military families, asserting that the removal of race- and gender-related books and curricula violated students’ First Amendment protections against government censorship.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boasberg moves to hold Trump administration in contempt over deportation flights
item.description: A federal judge found probable cause Wednesday to hold the Trump administration in criminal contempt for willfully disobeying his order to immediately halt deportations under the rarely used Alien Enemies Act and turn around any airborne planes. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4

item.title: Newsom says California will sue over Trump tariffs
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom and Attorney General Rob Bonta announced plans to sue the Trump administration over recent tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Man in tactical gear attacks officers with pepper spray at Boston-area courthouse, video shows
item.description: Police in a Boston suburb have arrested a man who they say entered a courthouse while wearing a gas mask and tactical gear and attacked several people with pepper spray.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Gambian ex-soldier convicted at US trial of torturing suspected backers of a failed 2006 coup
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former member of Gambia's military was convicted in federal court Tuesday of torturing five people accused of involvement in a failed coup against the West African country's longtime dictator nearly 20 years ago, capping a rare prosecution in the United States for torture committed a
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Santa Fe sheriff releases body camera footage of Gene Hackman investigation
item.description: Newly-released body camera footage shows the confusion and questions investigators had after actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were found dead in their home.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, WGN 9

item.title: FL representative says he'll go to El Salvador if wrongly deported man isn't returned this week
item.description: U.S. House Representative Maxwell Frost (D-Fla.) says he'll join other Democrat lawmakers planning to go to El Salvador if a man mistakenly deported isn't returned this week.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: No jurors picked on first day of Harvey Weinstein’s #MeToo retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As jury selection began Tuesday in Harvey Weinstein’s New York City rape retrial, some prospective jurors made clear they couldn't be fair in judging the one-time Hollywood mogul-turned-#MeToo pariah.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Actor William Levy arrested after drunken 'disturbance' at Florida restaurant: report
item.description: Cuban-American actor William Levy was arrested in South Florida Monday after causing a "disturbance" inside a restaurant, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man allegedly held captive by stepmother in Connecticut releases first public statement
item.description: In his first public statement, the man allegedly held captive in his home for over 20 years by his stepmother says he's been getting stronger as he begins the process of reclaiming his life.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: College football trophy comes apart in Vance's hands at White House
item.description: The College Football Playoff National Championship Trophy came apart during a White House visit by the Ohio State Buckeyes football team Monday as Vice President JD Vance tried to pick it up.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Phone calls, police interview footage shared during Lori Vallow Daybell murder trial
item.description: PHOENIX (ABC4) -- The Idaho mother convicted of killing her two children returned to the courtroom Monday on charges of conspiracy to murder her husband, Charles Vallow. Lori Vallow Daybell is representing herself in Maricopa County during this trial.
item.dc:creator: Sorina Trauntvein, ABC 4

item.title: New York plane crash claims lives of high-achieving family celebrating a birthday
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — When they boarded a small plane bound for upstate New York this past weekend, the six passengers — a pair of close-knit siblings, their parents and partners — were on the cusp of a new chapter in a family life already replete with personal and professional accomplishment.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Reported intruder arrested near UnitedHealthcare headquarters months after CEO's killing
item.description: Police in the Minneapolis suburb of Minnetonka said the suspect was arrested outside of the United Healthcare corporate campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Stocks rally worldwide after Trump eases some of his tariffs on electronics, for now
item.description: Stocks rose worldwide Monday after President Donald Trump relaxed some of his tariffs, for now at least, and as stress from within the U.S. bond market seems to be easing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Boxing legend George Foreman remembered for his love of family, faith and cheeseburgers
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — George Foreman was remembered Monday in a memorial service in his hometown of Houston for his legendary boxing career as well as for his love of God, family, horses and cheeseburgers and for his desire to help his fellow man. “He preached love all the time.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, Fox 5
